response.justification: Wei Chen is an experienced full-stack and web engineer with a strong track record across many languages and domains, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role on multiple critical axes. Most importantly, the candidate has no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a hard requirement listed explicitly — speaking only English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond that, the role demands deep C/Linux kernel expertise and embedded networking security knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal, L2/L3 switching), none of which appear meaningfully in the resume; the candidate's experience is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js web and data work. A brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis using Linux/C++ do not constitute the embedded firmware depth required.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience demonstrating adaptability and technical range across web, mobile, and embedded contexts
Some low-level systems exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments and embedded/hardware development at Altom Consulting with robotic arms
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based dev) and open-source community contributions showing engineering discipline
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a non-negotiable hard requirement for this role; candidate lists English, Romanian, and basic German only
No discernible experience with network security technologies required by the role (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal, L2/L3 switching, NETCONF, SNMP)
C programming and Linux kernel expertise are minimal and dated; the dominant tech stack is TypeScript/Node.js/web, incompatible with embedded firmware development
